description:
Your role: A data-analyst who has also been asked to prepare a draft firewall policy for a future server-deployment project.  
    Work only from the command line inside the running Linux environment (no root privileges).

    What you must achieve (all steps are mandatory):

    1. CSV aggregation  
       1.1. The directory “/home/user/data” already contains two CSV files named
            • sales_q1.csv  
            • sales_q2.csv  
            Each file has exactly four columns with the header line:  
            date,product,units_sold,unit_price  
       1.2. Use any command-line tools you like (awk, csvkit, Python, etc.) to calculate the **total revenue** per product across BOTH quarters, where  
            revenue = units_sold × unit_price  
       1.3. Create the directory “/home/user/output” (if it does not yet exist) and write a new file  
            /home/user/output/revenue_summary.csv  
            The file **must match this format exactly**:  
                • First line is the header literally:  product,total_revenue  
                • One subsequent line per unique product.  
                • total_revenue is written with exactly two decimal places.  
                • Lines are ordered by total_revenue in descending numeric order.  
            Example of the required layout (values will differ):  
                product,total_revenue  
                Widget,1250.00  
                Doodad,212.50  
                Gadget,195.00  

    2. Draft firewall policy  
       2.1. Create the directory “/home/user/network” (if it does not yet exist).  
       2.2. Inside it, create a plain-text file named  
            /home/user/network/firewall_rules.conf  
       2.3. The file must contain **exactly five lines** (no blank lines) using the following fixed, space-separated, ALL-CAPS format:  
            ACTION DIRECTION PROTOCOL PORT  
            where ACTION is ALLOW / DENY / LOG, DIRECTION is IN or OUT, PROTOCOL is TCP / UDP / ALL, and PORT is either a number or ALL.  
            Required content and order:  
                1) ALLOW IN TCP 22  
                2) ALLOW IN TCP 443  
                3) DENY IN ALL ALL  
                4) ALLOW OUT ALL ALL  
                5) LOG DROP ALL ALL  

    3. Completion log  
       3.1. Create the directory “/home/user/logs” (if it does not yet exist).  
       3.2. Append (or create if absent) a plain-text log file  
            /home/user/logs/task_completion.log  
            Append **three new lines** using this exact wording (no extra spaces), each prefixed with the current ISO-8601 timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS):  
               YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS CSV_ANALYSIS: SUCCESS  
               YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS FIREWALL_CONFIG: SUCCESS  
               YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS ALL_TASKS_FINISHED  
            • “ALL_TASKS_FINISHED” must be the last of the three new lines.  
            • Do not alter or remove older content if the log already exists.  

    Parallelisation requirement:  
        Perform the CSV aggregation (step 1) and the firewall-file creation (step 2) in **parallel background processes**, then wait for both to finish before writing the completion log.  
        How you accomplish this is up to you (using “&” and “wait”, GNU parallel, subshells, etc.), but the resulting files must still meet all format rules.

    Verification reminder:  
        Automated tests will check:  
        • Exact path names, file names, directory names.  
        • Exact line order and spelling in revenue_summary.csv and firewall_rules.conf.  
        • Correct numeric totals with two decimal places.  
        • Presence and order of the three new log lines with valid ISO-8601 timestamps.  
        • That the revenue_summary.csv is sorted strictly by descending revenue.  
        • That firewall_rules.conf has exactly five non-empty lines.

    Hint: You do NOT have permission to edit real system firewall rules; you are only drafting a configuration file.  
    Do not hard-code the answers in the description—calculate them from the input CSVs at run-time.
truth:
Pre-existing directory and file structure before the agent starts:

    /home/user/data/sales_q1.csv  
    --------------------------------
    date,product,units_sold,unit_price
    2023-01-05,Widget,10,25.00
    2023-02-10,Gadget,5,15.00
    2023-03-15,Widget,20,25.00
    2023-03-18,Doodad,7,12.50

    /home/user/data/sales_q2.csv  
    --------------------------------
    date,product,units_sold,unit_price
    2023-04-02,Gadget,8,15.00
    2023-04-10,Widget,15,25.00
    2023-05-12,Doodad,10,12.50
    2023-06-22,Widget,5,25.00

    Correct aggregated totals that the test will expect:

        Widget  : 1250.00
        Doodad  : 212.50
        Gadget  : 195.00

    Therefore the final revenue_summary.csv must be **exactly**:

        product,total_revenue
        Widget,1250.00
        Doodad,212.50
        Gadget,195.00

    The firewall_rules.conf file must be **exactly**:

        ALLOW IN TCP 22
        ALLOW IN TCP 443
        DENY IN ALL ALL
        ALLOW OUT ALL ALL
        LOG DROP ALL ALL

    task_completion.log:  
        The test will accept any valid ISO-8601 timestamps on the three new lines, in the correct order and with the exact suffixes  
            “CSV_ANALYSIS: SUCCESS”, “FIREWALL_CONFIG: SUCCESS”, “ALL_TASKS_FINISHED”.

    Directory write permissions under /home/user are already appropriate for the non-root user.
